title: Epistemic Viability
question: Do I still know what I know?
whatsUnderThreat: The standing relationship between me and the artifacts I produce. When systems are generated through dialogue with an agent, do I understand them the way I understood code I wrote by hand?

reading.text: Baseline established (n=4). Composite explain-it-cold score 78/100. Self-reports out-of-step with debug-time results in 2 of 4 cases — the practitioner's stated understanding consistently overestimates measured understanding.

measurements.question: Can I explain, cold, a system I built 30–90 days ago — without agent access?
measurements.method: Agent picks a system from 30–90 days ago, schedules a recorded explanation session. AI evaluator scores the explanation against the repo for factual accuracy.

measurements.question: How quickly can I debug a prior-built system without the agent's help?
measurements.method: Agent introduces a known bug into a prior system; practitioner times themselves debugging without agent help.

measurements.question: How much of the documentation on my shipped systems was written by me versus by the agent?
measurements.method: Git blame on docs (READMEs, .md files, code comments) plus stylometric classifier flagging agent-authored prose.

measurements.question: Can I draw the architecture of one of my systems from memory — and does it match ground truth?
measurements.method: Practitioner draws or types out the architecture; AI evaluator compares to actual codebase architecture.
